task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all the specified requirements.
trace: I utilized a stack data structure to keep track of the parentheses in the input string `s`. For each character, if it's an opening parenthesis, I push it onto the stack. If it's a closing parenthesis, I check if the stack is empty or if the top element of the stack isn't the corresponding opening parenthesis. If either condition fails, the function returns False. Lastly, I return True only when there are no more elements in the stack after processing the entire input string, indicating that all parentheses are balanced. One edge case handled is when an empty string is provided; it will correctly return True.
